entry.title: Re: Need help Updating PDF content through CPI
entry.content:
Hi, You need to create Payload in multiform data and send the same to Endpoint URL.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/lets-send-multipart-form-data-to-external-receiver-cpi-to-ai-app/ba-p/13694334

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sending-form-data-from-sap-integration-suite-best-practices-and-techniques/ba-p/13779024

above blogs may help

with regards

Siva

entry.title: Position Copy Permission - Recruiter
entry.content:
Hi Community,

One of my customers has this following ask. We have a Recruiter field in while creating position. The requirement here is that for each position, only the recruiter whose name is specified in the "Recruiter Name" field will have permission to edit that specific position.

For example, if the "Recruiter Name" for Position Code 1000 is mentioned as Rashmi, only Rashmi will have the permission to edit that position. PFA the screenshot.

Is it feasible to achieve, or would any workaround be really helpful?

Let me know if you have any questions or need further clarification.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Handling mandatory fields in a multi-country environment in SuccessFactors Recruiting
entry.content: Excellent.. Thanks for sharing your knowledge

entry.title: Re: Scheduling agreement flexible workflow on approval ,T...
entry.content: For the part to restart the WF only if there is 10% increase in amount, were you able to find any BADI or enhancement, for scheduling agreement WF i am not able to find any BADI to add this condition.

entry.title: Feasibility of Workflow in Document Generation in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I hope you're doing well.

One of my customers has this following ask. Is it feasible to set up a workflow during the document generation process in SuccessFactors. Specifically, if a workflow can be triggered or configured while generating documents in employee profile. PFA the screenshot.

Is it feasible to achieve, or would any workaround be really helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Creation of Production Order using Recipe process
entry.content: Hii Team, I am at waste management Industries facing issue to Weighting Average for BOM or recipe preparation, We have multiple RM for 1 FG , but before starting production Process, we have to select manual BOM through weighting avg formula of the MIC's which are recorded at the time of GR for incoming material. Currently, there is customized T-Code for the process. But we want to process it in SAP Best Practice or in SAP standard way. Please suggest process which includes same. Your suggestion will be highly appreciated. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Can the client mantain the data of "Output invoice bo...
entry.content:
您好，

您提到的配置點 102326 "指派號碼範圍至營業處" 目前暫無對應的終端用戶應用 App，其涉及銷售開票、財務憑證等跨模組核心流程憑證，故其操作權限需要謹慎處理。基於當前系統設計，您可以考慮參照 Process Navigator -> SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition -> Implementation -> Configurator apps，該 Excel 表單 “IMG Activity TRAN in BC” 中可在 F 列按 SSCUI ID（與 CBC 配置步驟 ID 一致）搜尋您的目標配置點，找到 A 列即為控制特定配置點的權限業務目錄，僅將需要開放給終端業務用戶的配置點的權限業務目錄分派給對應的業務用戶角色，從而實現業務用戶可訪問 CBC/SSCUI、但僅可調整/查看特定配置點的需求。

期望調整配置點為 Client independent（各系統環境單獨配置，不傳輸），請將需求詳細反饋至 SAP Customer Influence Portal。您在本問題中提及的需求目前已有類似反饋，請參閱 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/315906/?section=sectionDetails。

順頌時祺，
Fancy

entry.title: Re: Parametrization Variables in SAC
entry.content:
Hi 

Steps to Join Forecast and Price in an Optimized Story:

1. Create a Price Model: Import a table with `Course_Type` and `Price` as a separate model.

2. Add Both Models: Add the Forecast model and Price model to your optimized story.

3. Link Models: Use Model Linking to link `Course_Type` in both models.

4. Create a Calculation: Add a calculated measure for total price using this formula:

Total Price = Enrollment * Linked(Price)

5. Display Data: Add a table to show the forecast and total price side-by-side.

 

or try this way

Approach: Use Linked Models to Create a Dynamic Join in the Story
1. Prepare the Price Table Model:

Upload the price data as a separate model:
Course_Type Price (€)
Bachelor’s 300
Master’s 1,000
Postgraduate 800

Ensure Course_Type in this model matches the dimension in the forecast model.

2. Link the Models in the Story:

Open the optimized story.
Add both the Forecast Table and the Price Table models to the story.
Click on the Model Linking option in the story toolbar.
Create a linked dimension between the Course_Type field in both models.

3. Create a Calculation in the Story:

Add a new table or chart to the story, pulling data from the Forecast model.
Add a calculated measure for total price using the formula:

Total Price = Enrollment * Linked([Price Table].[Price])

Use the formula editor to reference the linked dimension and price measure from the Price Table model.

entry.title: Re: aFacetValueIds is Null error in SAC transport
entry.content:
Hi Pranav,

we are facing the same error since the QRC4/2024 Update. "aFacetValueIds is null". We can not import folders and files (story's, ...). 

We also opened a case within the SAP Support but there is no answer or solution until day! 

Best Regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: DO document flow status remains In process , even after the billing document is created
entry.content: Sorry you have to provide more information: which document oder, delivery... you are talking.

entry.title: Re: How to create a software component in S/4HANA on-premise?
entry.content:
Thank for the question and the answer 🙂

Is there any reason not to use the predefined software components delivered in the system (e.g. ZLOCAL shown here which is configured for cloud-ready development)? Alternatively, what is the advantage of creating your own?

EDIT: Ok, never mind, I guess the fact that those are flagged as local is the problem! 😄

entry.title: Unable to pass a parameter dynamically into a fiori URL via mashup in C4C
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We were trying to create a fiori URL mashup and were trying to pass a parameter dynamically with reference to the standard mashup SM00039 

But there is no provision to this via the mashup authoring in  UI configuration in C4C.

In the SDK as well we couldn't find any option to achieve this.

Could you please help us with your insights

What's happening in SM00039 ?

1. It's the 'Customer 360' standard mashup which is available on the Account workcenter.

2. When you click on action "Customer 360 View" .. it invokes the above mashup in a new tab

3. When invoking it passes the "External ID" field from Account object into the fiori app

What are we trying to achieve ?

1. Create a similar mashup with fiori app URl

2. And pass a parameter into the fiori app, the app loads after the search is filtered on the passed parameter

PFA screenshots for your reference.


Thanks and Regards
Krishna

entry.title: SAP Java NetWeaver list application java process details
entry.content:
Hi, 
I have installed SAP Java NetWeaver and when i run ps aux | grep java , it shows only the jstart process. There are many java applications running in the AS Java , how do i get the process details of those applications ?

entry.title: Re: SAP EMS objects
entry.content:
Hi Sam，

It is advisable for entitlements created in the EMS system to have corresponding objects or records in the upstream SAP S/4HANA system to ensure seamless management and fulfillment of those entitlements. This approach aligns with the best practices for SAP S/4HANA and helps in maintaining data consistency and operational efficiency across the enterprise. Esbetially would be helpful if you have upsell / downsell / change inside upstream, the auto generation integration content could listen to event of these document and affect entitlement EMS system accordingly.     

Br, Lambert Chen

entry.title: Re: Companion content not visible in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hello Anton, thank you for your reply! I checked the console and indeed found some error messages about Enable Now (see attachment). I think it has something to do with the config on SF? Or is this something we can fix on the side of Enable Now? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Request for Free Certification Support
entry.content:
Dear David,
 
My name is Yazan Omar Albarbarawi. I am writing to express my interest in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I am impressed by SAP's dedication to providing opportunities for underrepresented individuals in the technology field.
 
I am currently looking to switch careers mid-life and am particularly interested in gaining expertise in SAP FICO. However, my financial situation makes it difficult for me to afford the certification exam.
 
I would like to request the opportunity to take the SAP FICO certification exam free of charge as part of the Digital Skills Initiative. I am fully committed to the learning process and excited about the prospect of becoming SAP-certified.
 
Thank you for considering my request. I look forward to your response.
 
Sincerely,

entry.title: Delivery items DSO in BW doesn't contain movement type 602?
entry.content: We are using the standard SAP extractor 12lis__12_vcitm for extracting delivery items from SAP ECC system to BW system. There are no filters in the PSA layer or any visible ones in the extractor, however there are no items that have the BWART (movement type) 602 - Delivery cancellation (601 reversal). How can we change this?

entry.title: Migration Object EWM Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,Regarding data migration, we usually (on-premise and private) use:/scwm/isu: ewm stock/scwm/sbup: storage binAssign fixed bin (is there a massive?)Which are the related ones in Public cloud?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration Object EWM Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Please refer below SAP help portal page:

https://help.sap.com/docs/DATAMIG_S4HANA/d5aa3caabab04bb68d9cc3352d003330/b8d3c4714d324d3182532a3f6ce63abb.html?locale=en-US

At current release CE2408, there are 3 migration objects for EWM - Warehouse Stock, Warehouse Storage Bin and Warehouse Storage bin Sorting.

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Inventory budget
entry.content: Yeah, it would be much greate if can control budget checks in the order creation. Seems still can not fulfill?

entry.title: Re: Companion content not visible in SuccessFactors
entry.content: According to the console, SAP Enable Now is blocking CORS request from your SF system. In SAP Enable Now Manager got to Administration - Server Settings, Security and Passwords. Find the "Enable CORS" setting and switch it ON. In the CORS-allowed sites field put *

entry.title: Re: Duplication in component tab of maintenance order whi...
entry.content: Problem is resolved by implementing the Note 3543478. This note is shared with us by SAP after creating OSS. So once this note is available on support portal, you can use it to resolve the issue.

entry.title: Re: Generate data auto for iDOC testing
entry.content: Google search engine is a good tool. https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/wp-plu-not-triggering-from-wpma/qaq-p/653114

entry.title: Operate Your SAP Solution with the Power of SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-evolving digital landscape, organizations increasingly rely on robust tools to ensure seamless IT operations and achieve business continuity. SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management) has emerged as a transformative solution for managing operations across SAP and non-SAP systems. This blog explores how SAP Cloud ALM for Operations supports consultants, project managers, and leaders by providing end-to-end observability, automation, and actionable insights tailored to modern IT landscapes.

1. SAP Cloud ALM Overview

SAP Cloud ALM bridges the gap between IT operations and business processes. Designed to support hybrid environments, it delivers operational transparency, enabling organizations to monitor system health, automate tasks, and resolve issues efficiently.

Key highlights:

  • Supports both SAP and non-SAP systems.
  • Integrates monitoring, alerting, and analytics.
  • Ensures a unified approach for IT and business users.

2. Why Choose SAP Cloud ALM?

With around 3,000 active customers and significant monthly metrics like 25 billion health checks and 30 million alerts, SAP Cloud ALM demonstrates its scalability and reliability. Key benefits include:

  • Comprehensive coverage for cloud and on-premise systems.
  • Centralized monitoring for outages and maintenance.
  • Proactive alerting and event correlation.

3. Business Process Monitoring

Business Process Monitoring ensures operational transparency by tracking key business process KPIs. This empowers teams to detect disruptions, resolve anomalies, and maintain continuity.

Key features:

  • Predefined KPIs for swift implementation.
  • Alerts for process disruptions with drill-down capabilities.
  • Automated problem resolution.

4. Integration & Exception Monitoring

Integration & Exception Monitoring guarantees reliable data exchange across systems. It correlates messages across interfaces and provides insights into exceptions.

Key highlights:

  • Supports SAP and non-SAP integrations.
  • Unified monitoring for peer-to-peer and orchestrated interfaces.
  • Embedded alerting and issue analysis.

5. Real User Monitoring

Real User Monitoring evaluates system performance from an end-user perspective. This tool uncovers performance bottlenecks and errors by tracking both front-end and server-side activities.

Benefits:

  • Transparency into real-time user experiences.
  • Correlation of user requests with system responses.
  • Root cause identification for performance issues.

6. Synthetic User Monitoring

This feature simulates user interactions to proactively detect performance and availability issues. It offers continuous 24/7 monitoring using automated scripts.

Advantages:

  • Early detection of issues before impacting users.
  • Standardized metrics for performance trends.
  • Compatibility with various web applications.

7. Job & Automation Monitoring

Job & Automation Monitoring simplifies job execution and task management, ensuring operational efficiency.

Capabilities:

  • Tracking job status, delays, and execution times.
  • Historical data analytics for trend identification.
  • Embedded alerting for timely notifications.

8. Configuration & Security Analysis

This feature validates system configurations to ensure compliance with organizational standards.

Key aspects:

  • Automated change analysis for cloud and on-prem systems.
  • Pattern-based search capabilities.
  • Planned enhancements for TO-BE vs. AS-IS comparisons.

9. Health Monitoring

Health Monitoring assesses the technical health of systems and applications through KPIs, metrics, and alerts.

Highlights:

  • Light-weight monitoring for ABAP systems and SAP SaaS services.
  • Embedded analytics to detect trends.
  • Integration with corrective action tools.

10. Intelligent Event Processing and Notification Management

Event processing and notification management streamline alert routing and task assignments.

Key functionalities:

  • Unified event handling for manual and automated triggers.
  • Centralized alert inbox for monitoring use cases.
  • Integration with external task systems like Jira and ServiceNow.

11. External API Management and Operations Automation

SAP Cloud ALM integrates external APIs and automation engines to enable advanced operational workflows.

Use cases:

  • Automating responses to system events.
  • Exposing mass data to analytics platforms like Grafana.
  • Integration with SAP Automation Pilot and SAP Build Process Automation.

12. Analytics & Intelligence

Embedded analytics allow teams to analyze performance trends, identify critical events, and predict anomalies using AI-driven tools.

Key advancements:

  • Change point detection in real-user monitoring.
  • Dynamic thresholds in synthetic monitoring.
  • Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud.

13. Business Service Management

Business Service Management (BSM) aligns IT metrics with business objectives, ensuring seamless service delivery.

Key features:

  • Unified handling of SAP and customer-initiated events.
  • Service-level Availability (SLA) tracking.
  • Embedded reporting for business visibility.

Conclusion

SAP Cloud ALM for Operations is revolutionizing IT and business process management for SAP customers worldwide. With its comprehensive monitoring, intelligent automation, and actionable insights, it empowers organizations to optimize operations and achieve digital transformation. Whether you are a Solution Manager Consultant, Basis expert, or SAP leader, SAP Cloud ALM offers a clear pathway to operational excellence.

Let’s continue to explore the future possibilities of SAP Cloud ALM!

Keep Learning and Keep Sharing !

entry.title: How to use company currency for supplement budget process
entry.content:

The client uses THB base currency, I use manage budget document-project for supplement budgeting.

Question:

My project currency is THB.

Thanks,

May

entry.title: Re: ODATA loading only column headers no data
entry.content:
Hi,

Funny, i had the same behavior yesterday. In my case i had the Webi report configured with "Refresh on Open" - disabling this option then made the data to appear in Excel again. I run 4.3 SP04 P08.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano and fellow SAP learners.

My name is Hammad Ali, and I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to @David_Chaviano and the SAP team for their incredible efforts in launching the Digital Skills Initiative. This initiative has been immensely beneficial, providing access to high-quality resources and empowering learners like me to upskill effectively.

I am thrilled to have completed the "SAP Learning Journey Developing with SAP Integration Suite" program under the Digital Skills Initiative, and I am eager to leverage the opportunity to take a free certification attempt. This program is a shining testament to SAP's unwavering commitment to nurturing learning, growth, and innovation within the tech community.



Please accept my warmest kudos to @David_Chaviano and the entire SAP team for driving such impactful initiatives and making a lasting difference in the lives of learners worldwide. Your dedication, expertise, and passion for education are truly inspiring, and I feel grateful to be a part of this journey.

Best Regards,
Hammad Ali.

#SAP #DigitalSkillsInitiative

entry.title: Re: Contact and product data references in the Emarsys sc...
entry.content:
If it is still relevant,

For contacts, it is simply {{ contact.fieldnumber }}.
Ex. {{contact.1}} is the first name.
{{contact.37|required}} will be the mobile number and will send the message only if the value isn't empty.

You can know the field number from Management>Field Editor 

For products, it depends on the source. If it is from a Webextend cart event or Personalization Rules catalogue. It changes the opening {% foreach %} statement.
It is a good idea to take example from the Abandoned Cart block in the master template. 

Hope it helps

entry.title: Re: Field DELI_SERV_TYPE & DELI_SERV_NUMBER of Entity AD_...

The reason for my post was if anyone knows why the fields are grayed out. I will find out the reason and update here, which might be useful for someone else in the future.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Output Management: How can an output from DEV/TEST be marked as test print?
entry.content:
Is it possible in the SAP S/4HANA public cloud to define the output of documents generated from development system or test system are marked as test prints? 

We are thinking of a watermark or similar.

We want to prevent printouts from the test or DEV system from being mistakenly circulated.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Output Management: How can an output from DEV/TEST be marked as test print?
entry.content:
Is it possible in the SAP S/4HANA public cloud to define the output of documents generated from development system or test system are marked as test prints? 

We are thinking of a watermark or similar.

We want to prevent printouts from the test or DEV system from being mistakenly circulated.

But, how can we achieve creating a S4  warranty service contract from warranty entitlement in EMS? Is there any standard way or need to a totally custom development?

 

Thanks and regards

Sam

entry.title: Try sending data in multipart/form-data format on SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
*This is a technical verification article that is not yet known if it can be put into actual use.

There is a rare problem in using SAP Build Apps. That is that SAP Build Apps does not support multipart/form-data format communication. Although this format has been requested less and less recently, it is still in use, so I thought it might be possible to use it and tried to implement it.

Current SAP Build Apps

Recently, the existing REST API Direct Integration has been replaced by universal REST API integration. I personally think that the HTTP Method is fixed for each action such as Select/Create, but compared to REST API Direct Integration, where it was easy to give arguments, there are some things that make me think, “Hmmm...” However, since the HTTP Method can be specified within an action such as Select/Create, it is no longer the case that “Select is used to insert data,” which is a good thing. However, since the HTTP Method can be specified within Select/Create and other actions, it is no longer the case that “Select is used to insert data”, which is a good thing.

HTTP Header and BODY can be manipulated to some extent manually. It would have been nice to be able to choose to send data in multipart/form-data format here, but unfortunately it is not an option.

Brief description of the format multipart/form-data

You should read the RFC specification for this, but here is a “super” simple explanation

  • Specify “multipart/form-data; boundary=<boundary string>” as Content-Type in Header.
  • In the BODY, specify the following

--<boundary string>
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="<Field name>"

<Field value>
--<boundary string>
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="<Field name>"

<Field value>
--<boundary string>--

  • The boundary string must be preceded by “--” and followed by “--” at the end.
  • The same string as the BOUNDARY string must not appear in this entire sentence.
  • Use CR+LF for line feed code.
  • A single line break should be inserted between the Content-Disposition and the value of the field.

are available. (Of course there are others! Be sure to check the RFC specifications!)

The key point is "The same string as the boundary string must not appear in this entire sentence". When data is submitted from a browser using an HTML form, this process is done automatically and a boundary string that does not appear in the data is automatically created, but this time you must do it yourself. In particular, this multipart/form-data format can be used to upload file data. You will need to be careful in this case.

How to submit data in multipart/form-data format in SAP Build Apps?

The following is an example. It is based on the motif of submitting the following HTML form. The destination is https://httpbin.org/post, which is a useful service for this kind of situation.

 

<form action="https://httpbin.org/post" method="post">
<input type="text" name="Field1">
<input type="text" name="Field2">
</form>

 

1. Integration settings

Use the Universal REST API Integraion.

Set the two text items that are boundary and form input items as Additional Input.

Use Create as the method, making sure POST is set as the Request Method.

Create "Content-Type" as Request Headers.

Value should be set as follows so that the Boundary set for Additional Input is used.

 

"multipart/form-data; boundary=" + query.additionalInputs.boundary

 

This is the key : Request body mapper setting.

 

"--"+query.additionalInputs.boundary + "
" + "Content-Disposition: form-data; name="Field1"

"+query.additionalInputs.TextField1+ "
--" + query.additionalInputs.boundary + "
" + "Content-Disposition: form-data; name="Field2"

"+query.additionalInputs.TextField2+ "
--" + query.additionalInputs.boundary +"--"

 

You can get a general idea of what this actually looks like from the Exsample results in the screenshot. The point is that the line breaks are done with CR+LF (= ).
If you understand this formula, you will be able to handle any form.

Enter appropriate values in the test and run it.

As mentioned above, boundary is a delimiter in the data set in the body, so it must not duplicate the data entered in the form. (I use a random keyboard clattering.)

It seems that https://httpbin.org/post recognized this as form data.

2. Logic building

When actually incorporating this into the logic, let's say the screen is constructed as follows

Set the following as the logic for the button.
Assign GUID to Boundary for now.

*As mentioned above, it is necessary to check if there is any problem using GUIDs. I think you need to check if this generated GUID value is not included in the value entered in the form, and if it is found to be included, you need to generate the GUID value again and check again .... If it is found to contain a GUID value, the logic should be to generate the GUID value again and check it again.

"Create Record" assigns boundary and form values as parameters.

OK, Let's try.

Set a value on the form and run it.

The value was recognized as a form value = Send this data as "multipart/form-data".

 

Not a very cool way to do it, but for now I was able to send data in multipart/form-data format with SAP Build Apps. I hope this will be of some help.

entry.title: Re: How to check ATP results for multiple items on one sc...

entry.title: Re: Setting up the Target Population for Delegator in Delegation for Performance Reviews
entry.content:

I also have the same question/issue as yours - were you able to set up the delegation feature properly? 

Thanks and Regards,

Kat

entry.title: Change of NumberFormat Behavior for Currencies with UI5 version 1.130
entry.content:
Intro

With a recent change in UI5 version 1.130, we corrected the formatting of decimal places for currencies.

We now differentiate between two cases:

  • Case 1:  If the style: "long"/"short" format option is not set, and no decimals are provided in the  oFormatOptions of the sap.ui.core.format.NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance API (either via the direct format option decimals or via currency customizing  as specified by the format option customCurrencies), the given value is formatted with the decimals value defined by the CLDR standard.

  • Case 2:  If the style: "long"/"short" format option is set, only the decimals explicitly set in the oFormatOptions of the sap.ui.core.format.NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance API, are considered  for the determination of the number of decimal places in the output.
    Any other source for the decimals attribute (i.e. your currency customizing or the CLDR) is not considered anymore.

Case 1: Format option style: "long"/"short" isn't set

What's different?

Previously,  applications may have defined currency formats, which relied on the maxFractionDigits format option to specify the number of decimal places. This is incorrect usage, as the maxFractionDigits parameter is meant to define the maximum but not the actual number of decimals.

Example - Prior to the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123.456", "USD"]
- Format options: {maxFractionDigits: 3}
- Formatted result: "123.456 USD"

 

With our change, this behavior has changed. Now the semantics of the maxFractionDigits/minFractionDigits format options is correctly interpreted for currency formats, namely as the maximum and minimum number of permitted decimals.

Example - After the chang:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123.456", "USD"]
- Format options: {maxFractionDigits: 3}
- Formatted result: "123.46 USD" (rounding is applied)

 

For the en  locale, the formatted result has 2 decimal places:

  • The maxFractionDigits define a maximum of 3 decimal places
  • The CLDR data defines the USD currency in the en locale with 2 decimal places

As  2 < 3,  this is the correct result. The output does not exceed the maximum number of decimal places in this format, and the currency is formatted according to the locale-specific standard.

This change also affects the formatting of currencies with fewer decimal places than expected by their CLDR configurations.

Example - Prior to the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123.4", "USD"]
- Format options: {minFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123.4 USD"

 

In the prior behavior, the number of decimal places remained the same if the number given contained more decimal places than defined by the minFractionDigits option.

Example - After the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123.4", "USD"]
- Format options: {minFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123.40 USD"

 

With the new change in place, this is not the case anymore as the CLDR defines the number of decimal places for the USD currency in the given locale as 2. Therefore, a zero is padded at the end.

Case 2: Format option style: "long"/"short" is set

During the implementation of Case 1 it was discovered that the configuration of decimal places determined via any source (currency customizing or CLDR) is also applied to short or long formats of numbers (e.g. 1000.00 USD -> 1.00K USD). This is unnecessary for such formats; therefore, we have now disabled the application of any currency customzing if the format option style: "long"/"short" is set.

The only remaining way to apply decimal place formatting to short or long formats of currencies is the decimals format option.

What's different?

Previously, an application may have expected its currency short or long formats to have the number of decimal places as defined by the currency customizing for the currency, in its output format; this may no longer be the case.

Example - Prior to the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123456", "USD"]
- Format options: {style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123.00K USD"

 

This result is incorrect, as 2 zeros are padded at the end. The CLDR data determines the decimals format option to 2 for USD.  While this is correct for a format without style short or long, formatted numbers with style often contain a metric prefix like “K” so that using the currency’s decimals is wrong.

Example - After the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123456", "USD"]
- Format options: {style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123K USD"

 

With our new change, no decimal places are displayed in the output format.

A similar behavior can be observed when customCurrencies are provided.

Example - Prior to the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123456", "USD"]
- Format options: {
                      style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0, 
                      customCurrencies: {USD: {decimals: 2}}
                  }
- Formatted result: "123.00K USD"

 

Example - After the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123456", "USD"]
- Format options: {
                      style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0,
                      customCurrencies: {USD: {decimals: 2}}
                  }
- Formatted result: "123K USD"

 

It can be observed that the currency customizing is ignored if style: "long"/"short" is set.

To avoid any confusion, this rule does apply even if the currency amount is too small to contain a metric prefix (e.g. "K") in its output format.

Example - Prior to the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123", "USD"]
- Format options: {style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123.00 USD"

 

The given number too small to be formatted into a short format in the US locale.

Example - After the change:

 

- Values to be formatted: ["123", "USD"]
- Format options: {style: 'short', maxFractionDigits: 0}
- Formatted result: "123 USD"

 

With the new change in place, the currency customizing (in this case via the CLDR) is not applied.

What does that mean for tests and applications?

In case 1:

The new behavior of tests and applications depends on the application setup. If the currency formats either use currency customizing where decimals are defined for all currencies or define a decimals format option for their binding, the behavior stays the same.

If, on the other hand, an application relies on the maxFractionDigits option to always determine the number of decimal places on its currency formats, this cannot be assured anymore. Only if  maxFractionDigits < decimals may the maxFractionDigits option still be used to format the output.

Thus, it is up to the application developer to decide, between one of the following alternatives:

  • Alternative 1 (The application should rely on its currency customizing/the CLDR standard for the decimal places of currencies) - Any failing tests have to be adjusted.

  • Alternative 2 (The application should continue to determine the number of decimal places for currencies itself) - The application has to use the decimals format option instead of the maxFractionDigits option for that.

In case 2:

With style set to short or long, the same alternatives exist as before, but with a slightly different semantic, and it is still up to the application developer to decide:

  • Alternative 1 (The application shouldn't have the currency customizing/the CLDR standard affect its short or long formats by adding decimals) - Any failing tests have to be adjusted.

  • Alternative 2 (The application should continue to always display decimal places in its short or long formats) - The application has to use the decimals format option instead of the maxFractionDigits option for that.

For more information regarding the format options available for our NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance API, see: sap.ui.core.format.NumberFormat.getCurrencyInstance

entry.title: Production Order / BAPI
entry.content:
Hi,

There are an integration between MII and SAP.

Production order is created in SAP and then being sent to MII. MII can be change the item in the production order; but these changes are not reflected in SAP. For this reason we want to use BAPI. As a result if the one of the item delete in MII, changes reflect in SAP. 

There are BAPIs that we use below in a row.

CO_XT_COMPONENTS_DELETE
BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT
CO_XT_ORDER_PREPARE_COMMIT
BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT
CO_XT_ORDER_INITIALIZE
BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

After we used BAPI, on the CO02 screen items colours changed but there aren't any deleted (silindi) sign on the screen.

Also there aren't any deleted (silindi) signs about related items on the resb table.

How can I figure it out? Thank you

entry.title: Re: CSRF validation fails (403 Forbidden) from SAP Build Work Zone approuter
entry.content:
Hello @smarchesini, here is the content of the DetailsExt.controller.js file where the FileUploader behaviour is implemented. As noted earlier, handling CSRF token works without issues when running without the CF's approuter.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageBox"
], function (MessageBox) {
    "use strict";
    return  {

        onInit: function (oEvent) {

        },

        onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
            var oFileUploader = oEvent.getSource();
            var oFile = oFileUploader.oFileUpload.files[0];
            this._file = oFile;
            console.log("File selected:", oFile);

            /* Destroy the header before each new uplaod.
               Otherwise the parameters are just accumulating on each consecutive call
               and the CSRF tokens are not recognised by SAP Server and refused */
            oFileUploader.destroyHeaderParameters();

            var headerParmaSlug = new sap.ui.unified.FileUploaderParameter();
            headerParmaSlug.setName('slug');
            headerParmaSlug.setValue(oFileUploader.getValue());
            oFileUploader.addHeaderParameter(headerParmaSlug);

            /* Get the CSRF token value and add as new header parameter */           
            this.csrfToken = this.getView().getModel().getSecurityToken();
            var headerParmaCSRF = new sap.ui.unified.FileUploaderParameter();
            headerParmaCSRF.setName('x-csrf-token');
            headerParmaCSRF.setValue(this.csrfToken);
            oFileUploader.addHeaderParameter(headerParmaCSRF);

        },
        afterDialogClose (oEvent) {
            var oParameters = oEvent.getParameters()

        },
        onUploadStart:function () {

        },       
        onUploadPress: function () {
/*
        Not used, because FileUploader is set up to start uploading immediately 
        after choosing a file and the button is hidden 
*/
        },
        onUploadComplete: function (oEvent) {
            var sResponse = oEvent.getParameter("response");
            var sResponseRaw = oEvent.getParameter("responseRaw");
            var sStatus = oEvent.getParameter("status");
            if (sResponse) {
                MessageBox.show("Upload complete: " + sResponse);
                var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
                oModel.refresh(true);
            } else {
                MessageBox.show("Upload failed." + sResponseRaw );
            }
        }
    }
});

entry.title: Validating Employment, Job, and Compensation Information for a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment
entry.content:
When you create a higher duty or temporary assignment, you must define rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information. If these rule functions are not configured, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment.

Introduction

When you create a higher duty or temporary assignment, you must define rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

 If these rule functions are not configured, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment. With the higher duty or temporary assignment moving to service layer, these validations are carried out in the background. Therefore, if you have not configured these rule functions, you might have inconsistent data in your system and unexpected warnings.

For a higher duty or temporary assignment, you can configure three main validation rule functions.

 

Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Employment Information

This rule function validates the employment information and ensures that the higher duty assignment duration is within the boundary of the nominal employment dates, that only one higher duty exists for a nominal employment, and that there are no overlapping higher duty assignments. For more information about this validation, refer to Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Employment Information.

To define rules to validate employment information, refer to Define Rule to Validate Employment Information. This rule is triggered for back-end synchronization whenever the employment information of the nominal assignment changes and the user wants to update the employment information of the higher duty or temporary assignment with the data from Position fields defined for synchronization.

The rule function allows you to trigger the following validations:

  • The higher duty or temporary assignment can be active only in the duration the nominal employment is active.
  • If or when the nominal employment is terminated, the corresponding higher duty or temporary assignment must also be terminated.
  • Only one higher duty or temporary assignment can be active for a nominal employment at any given point in time.
  • Any changes to the employment information of a nominal employment or higher duty changes are validated against the start and end dates of the nominal employment assignments.

Behavior in Case of Errors

If the nominal employment has a corresponding higher duty assignment, then if you update the employment information of the nominal assignment, you get an error if the higher duty assignment duration is not within the boundary of the nominal employment dates.

If you try to update the higher duty assignment duration to a duration that is outside the boundary of the corresponding nominal assignment, then an error occurs, and the updated duration is not saved. You can modify the higher duty assignment duration to be within that of the corresponding nominal assignment. Alternatively, to make changes to both the nominal assignment and higher duty assignment durations, you can modify the nominal assignment duration first and then update the higher duty assignment duration to be within the nominal assignment duration.

 

Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Job Information

This rule function validates the job information and ensures that the company, country and employee class of the higher duty or temporary assignment is same as that of the corresponding nominal assignment. For more information about this validation, refer to Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Job Information.

To define rules to validate job information, refer to Define Rule to Validate Job Information.

This rule function validates that the company, country and employee class of the higher duty or temporary assignment is same as that of the corresponding nominal assignment. Using this rule function, you can ensure that any change to the company or country is not allowed if an active or future-dated higher duty assignment exists for the employee.

Behavior in Case of Errors

If the nominal employment has a higher duty assignment, then if the company, country and employee class of the higher duty or temporary assignment are not the same as that of the corresponding nominal assignment, then a warning occurs when you try to update the nominal assignment. It is recommended that you update the job information in the higher duty or temporary assignment accordingly.

If you make changes to the company, country and employee class of the higher duty or temporary assignment, an error occurs if the modified information does not match the information in the nominal assignment, and the job information is not updated. To ensure that the changes match the information in the nominal assignment, you must first update the job information in the nominal assignment.

 

Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Compensation Information

This rule function validates the compensation information and ensures that any change in the pay group of the nominal assignment is also updated in the compensation information of the currently active and future-dated higher duty or temporary assignments. You can also ensure that only a pay-component recurring of type Estimated HD allowance is present in the compensation information. For more information about this validation, refer to Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Compensation Information.

To define rules to validate compensation information, refer to Define Rule to Validate Compensation Information.

Using this rule function, you can ensure that any change in the pay group of the nominal assignment is also updated in the compensation information of the currently active and future-dated higher duty or temporary assignments. You can also ensure that only a pay-component recurring of type Estimated HD allowance can be present in the compensation information.

 

Behavior in Case of Errors

If the nominal employment has a higher duty assignment, then if any change in the pay group of the nominal assignment is not also updated in the compensation information of the currently active and future-dated higher duty or temporary assignments, a warning occurs. To prevent data inconsistencies, you must ensure that any change in the pay group of the nominal assignment is also updated in the compensation information of the currently active and future-dated higher duty or temporary assignments.

If you make any changes to the pay group information in the currently active and future-dated higher duty or temporary assignments, then an error occurs. You must first update the pay group information in the nominal assignment.

 

Using Planned End Date in Rules

If you want to have a rule on the planned end date for a higher duty or temporary assignment, you can use the following field.

 

Field for Adding Planned End Date for a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment

 

Summary

To summarize, we saw the importance of defining rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information for a higher duty ore temporary assignment. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

Configuring these rule functions is also important because these validations will happen in the background when higher duty or temporary assignment runs on the service layer and may trigger unexpected warnings. For more information about centralized services in Employee Central, refer to Centralized Services in Employee Central.

For more details about implementing higher duty or temporary assignment, check out the Implementing Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment guide.

Thank you for reading. I request you to share your feedback by leaving a comment.

entry.title: Allocation in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a scenario that I need help with. I upload data with a split on the members of [d/COUNTRY] and on [d/STORE] = "#". The same total is uploaded afterwords, but the other way around i.e. with a split on the members of [d/STORE] and [d/COUNTRY = "#". I now need to merge these two together with a Data Action while having almost the same original split on [d/COUNTRY] and [d/STORE].

Anyone have any ideas?

entry.title: deletion of validation can´t be transported with fiori app F4406 Manage Substitutions a. Validations
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we have created a validation rule in the Fiori App F4406 "Manage Substitutions and Validations for Financial Documents". (S/4 HANA 2023 private Cloud)
In testsystem, we found some issues with the validation and it needs to be deleted.

We´ve deleted the validation in development, but there was not prompt to save the deletion to a transport.

Transporting with t.code GTC9 doesn´t work, in SAP GUI this validations are not even displayed.

Does anyone know what how to transport the deletion?

thanks,

entry.title: Re: Transport from onpremise sap systems to RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

Please find below answers to your queries-

1. can we do client export on-premise and import to S/4HANA cloud,private edition? - Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document "SAP Client Operations" section.
2. Can we get the transfer request on-premise and import it into S/4HANA cloud,private edition?- Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document  "Transport Management section".
3. If there is a version difference between on-premise (2021) and S/4HANA cloud,private edition (2023), is it acceptable to import the transfer in unconditional mode? - Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document  "Transport Management section".

Regards,

Shilpi

entry.title: Israel: PDI BADI for Exclusion of Withholding Tax Items
entry.content:
In this blog, you learn how to exclude invoices, including payment and clearing documents, using Enhancement Implementation in SAP Cloud Applications Studio. This customization helps you exclude invoices that are not required for tax reporting in SAP Business ByDesign.

You need both the payment and clearing documents for tax reporting. You must decide whether to send both types of documents. In Israel, there are two types of business scenarios.

1. Scenario that requires only payment documents reported to the tax authority.

2. Scenario that requires both payment and clearing documents reported.

The current enablement of this feature meets the need of all Israel customers. To address this, PDI BADI offers an option that caters to both types. If you require only Payment documents, you can delete all Clearing documents and use only Payment documents for Tax Reporting. If you need both Payment and Clearing documents, you can use both for Tax Reporting.

To meet this requirement, the customer needs to implement an additional logic at the backend with the help of your respective partners. This logic deletes unwanted documents that are not considered for Tax Reporting. The step-by-step procedure below explains on how to implement the additional (Deletion) Logic.

1. In this example, two supplier invoices are created for a customer.

2. Two supplier charges are created against each of the two supplier invoices created in Step 1.

3. Perform “Manual Clearing” for the supplier charges against the corresponding supplier invoices.

A Partially Cleared line item appears as shown below.

4. Perform “Pay Manually by” Outgoing Bank Transfer to initiate the payment of 67 ILS.

We do a posting for the same.

Repeat step 3 and 4 to perform one more round of Manual Clearing and Pay Manually by” Outgoing Bank Transfer for the balance payment.

5. Initiate Withholding Tax (WHT) 856 Return by creating WHT Return Run.

In this, two records with UUID’s 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA4BD12BD533023 (UUID for clearing document) and 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA5223DC6433026 (UUID for the payment document) are passed. As per the logic in BAdI, all the Split items (clearing and the payment documents) are passed to the customer.

Let's assume that out of these two split items, you deleted one split item (clearing document) based on the newly implemented logic and report the other split item (payment document). Consider that you have deleted 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA4BD12BD533023 and sent 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA5223DC6433026. As per logic the system will consider only the record received from you for Tax Reporting purpose.

Below is the screenshot for the Tax Return ID generated.

If you check the Tax Return Tab, you can see that we had 2 records to be reported but due to customer/partner enhancement only one record was finally reported for the Tax Reporting purpose.

Export Parameters

CALL BADI lb_pdi_wht_return->delete_split_items

EXPORTING

is_input_data = ls_nodeid_tab

CHANGING

cs_result_data = lt_result.

LS_NODEID_TAB is the export parameter with all node IDs.

Customer will receive node IDs through the highlighted parameter.

Please find below the sample code:

Find below the sample ABSL code, which is placed in the Cloud Application Studio, tested with one UUID number and successfully deleted the same. 

Below are the technical details of the development and cloud application. 

S.No

Technical Name

Description

1

Namespace

https://sap.com/xi/AP/DueItemManagement/Global

2

Enhancement Option

DeleteWHTSplitItems

3

Enhancement Option Type

Multiple Use

4

Business Object

WithholdingTaxDeclaration

5

Structure

glo_il_uuid

6

Enhancement Spot

ES_GLO_PDI_IL_WHT_RETURN

7

BADI Definition

PDI_BADI_IL_WHT_RETURN

8

Interface

IF_BADI_IL_WHT_RETURN

9

Method

DELETE_SPLIT_ITEMS

The PDI BAdI handles both the below Israel scenarios for Withholding Tax Returns (856, 857, 102).

· Scenarios that require only Payment documents

· Scenarios that require Payment documents as well as Clearing documents.

For customers intend to use both payment and clearing documents for Tax Reporting purposes, the existing functionality will work as earlier and need not have to perform any new action.

entry.title: Report for Credit Risk Based impairment // SAP CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am currently in the Explore Phase of a SAP Cloud PE Project. The customer wants to valuate the open customer positions. Due to some features the advanced valuation has, we want to go with it instead of the classic valuation. 

Now we want to valuate the "Doubtful account valuation". For the valuation you use the further valuations App in the classic valuation. For Advanced valuation you use the credit risk based impairment (GL- JOB). The Revaluation of the Customer positions does work in the Job log. Now we want to analyze the valuation and the "allowance" per debitor. In the Classic valuation this is possible with the Doublful Account Valuation APP. Here you can see the open Positions per Customer on a ovedue- interval grid and the accumulated allowance as well as the percentage (allowance/OP). 

Sadly the "Allowance" Field is connected to the results of the "further valuations" App and the there used  valuation method 3. So my allowance, calculated in the GL Job is not displayed here: 

I definetly need a report that shows me the Open Positions of a customer per overdue- interval grid as well as the accumulated allowance amount per customer (Idealy also per document). Is there any Standard way of getting the same report with the advanced valuation as I can get with the classic valuation? 

If not, can I copy the Doubtful Accounts Valuation App and extend the report with a value field of the accumulated allowance amount? 

 

Otherwise, is it recommendable to use the classic valuation approach instead? We have no problem with that, but heard, that it might run out of SAP Support in the near/middle future? 

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Some user properties are mapped as SAML attributes by default. When a property is mapped, it can not be updated via the SCIM API, which will include user provisioning services. Simply unmap these properties. Go to SAP Analytics Cloud, as the system owner, Menu-Security-Users, and select the button to 'Map SAML attributes'. This opens a dialogue box allowing you to select which attributes to unmap (or remap if you've previously removed the mapping). The first name and last name are mapped attributes, as well as others. If you want these to be updated via the SCIM API rather than via SAML then you'll need to unmap them.

If this isn't your issue (or even if it is), you may find the sample Postman Collections helpful in validating whether the issue is at the SAP Analytics Cloud end or with your provisioning mapping service. The user guide also provides a little more detail on the mapped attributes mentioned above.

entry.title: Activate Generic Object Services for PR
entry.content:

Help Greatly appreciated. Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports
entry.content:
Hi @CSmith,

thank you very much for quick reply!

I've checked the lookup table and I have all currencies to GBP only. Nothing from GBP to other currencies. Table is from 2023 so some slight differences in currency rates can appear. 

Lookup table:

Story Report:

Currency Converter:

Is it possible to check why the system probably is using the inverse of the rate? I think, it should use data from lookup table.

I need in my Story for VRP currencies to GBP not the other way around.

Regards,

Aneta

entry.title: Re: TRM Treasury - Letter of Credit – Configuration
entry.content:
Very Good information!

One question, how we could generate invoice for fee which are being charged to inter-companies?

entry.title: Re: Vendor with Cust(XVERR) Field is not visible in UI in...
entry.content:
Hi , 

I was also facing this problem and then I found the SNOTE 3265032 from SAP where they have explained that with the code changes in note 2469063 it is a standard logic that field XVERR will be hidden in UI.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003265032

Further they have explained that as a workaround, after adding the corresponding field of "Clearing with Customer" or "Clearing with Vendor" in the corresponding UIBB, you should also add the corresponding LIFNR and KUNNR in the general data section of ERP Customer and ERP Vendor UIBB and then the function will work.

I have tested this and it works absolutely fine for us.

 

 

BR 

Ajaya Kumar Mishra

entry.title: Re: Simple CAP Application Deploy BTP (Trial) : Found no...
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the update. I can confirm it's working now. However when disabling/ enabling CF in my subaccount, the org name change xxx_ext and I was not able to list the orgs (CLI: cf orgs) anymore (no orgs available). I ended up reinstalling my BTP trial account, now it's working again.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow for Sales for Public Cloud - Complete Guide
entry.content: This is an excellent blog!!

entry.title: Re: CDS views that can be used to Distinguish fields chan...
entry.content: Hello, I already tried this CDS view but its not visible on Data Browser, nor are there any entries in them when i access it using eclipse. I am not able to use it in Create Custom CDS view also.

entry.title: How to fill item navigation property of standard API to create Supplier Invoice in S4 HANA CLoud
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working in S4 HANA public cloud and my requirement is to create Supplier Invoice by passing header data and navigation property "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" of API "/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/A_SupplierInvoice".

I have created service consumption model for this API in eclipse. But, in the CREATE operation of consumption model no code generated to navigate to "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" as I need to supply two fields here "ServiceEntrysheet" and ServiceEntryItem" as this is mandatory to POST supplier invoice.

I have successfully supplied the header data and when I execute the code, I am getting message "No Item data supplied to post invoice". This is because above navigation property is not supplied in the payload.

Kindly suggest how I can supply the data into "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" of this API.

I have attached my code for reference.

Many Thanks In advance!

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive: Execution order item and Billable maintenance order
entry.content:
One question, it is possible to have an account assignment on this billable maintenance order and to post this acc ass (for example an internal order) to the service order with advanced execution ?
Thanks Michaela

entry.title: How to post Deferred Expenses without Purchase Order // prepaid expenses and deferred charges
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am using a SAP Cloud Public Edition System. I am receiving Invoices from Vendors (no Purchas Order) that are for future Periods, but due today. I want to pay the invoices today and also pay the Input Tax today. As far as I understood the Standard Process is as follows: 

1. Poste the Vendor Invoice against a Deferral Account

2. Create a recurring journal document that Debits the expense account and credits the deferral account. 

Like this I have a Invoice due today, the tax is reported today, but the expense is deferred to another period. The problem with this is, that I do have to be aware of 2 seperat steps. The Invoices are received via Ariba and are transfered in the system automatically. I dont want anyone checking all the invoices, if they have to be deferred. Isnt there a one step process, where I can set the Period/Periods in which the expense actually accures in the invoice? 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: BRF+ E-Mail Output for RFQ - to the email address of the SAP user creating the RFQ
entry.content:
Dear experts, 

we have this requirement: when a Request for Quotation (RFQ) is created the email output via BRF+ should be sended to the email address of the SAP user which created the RFQ. 

How can we configure this requirement?

Thank you in advance

Hi,

The request needs to be addressed via an enhancement request through the SAP Customer Influence Portal. Currently, there's a similar open request: "Extend Customer Material (A2X) API with Long Text". You may check and vote for it.

Best Regards,
Fancy

Br,

Juan.

My Concern is only with the 'Relationships' option, do I need to manually add the history of Idocs for the respective PR ?

 

PFB the screenshot for reference

entry.title: IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Production system or Alternate options ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, All,

IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Production system or let me know Alternate options ?

 

As per user requirement, we have made 'Conditional Formatting' (If Key Figure Cell Value is NOT Equal to '0' (Zero) then display in Red color for the IBP Key Figure.

This color code Formatting is done in 'IBPFormattingSheet' for the Key Figure in 'IBP DEVELOPMENT System' and tested and working fine.

 

The Question is :

Do we need to do this IBPFormatting cell Color Code Formatting directly in Planning View Templates in IBP PRODUCTION system ?

Or is there any way/option to do in IBP Development system and do Transport or Export from Development system to Quality system and to Production system ?

 

Appreciate your help in this regards.

Thanks.

Parthi

entry.title: Call for ideas/volunteers/community drivers
entry.content:
Hello All,

How can we make our SAP Community in Georgia more active and open for discussions?

If you feel that you have some information to share, or may be some idea what can be done, please, do not hesitate to share in this group. If you would like to become a community driver, also, please, show up here.

I understand that everyone has very tight schedule, but let's share our thoughts, plans and achievements here.

We also need to think about SAP activities for the next year. It would be great if we organize SAP Inside Track in Tbilisi. Just to have an idea of this event, here are some Inside Tracks, held in 2024 in other countries:

SAP Inside Track Warsaw 2024 – Key Highlights, Ins... - SAP Community

Recap of SAP CodeJam and SAP Inside Track Vilnius ... - SAP Community

SAP Inside Track the Netherlands - #sitNL 2024 (ma... - SAP Community

So, let's speak up. Any news or idea from anyone is equally interesting - someone attended an interesting SAP event, someone has passed the critical project milestone, has interesting info, or solved a difficult case related to SAP project, anything...

Let's start talking to each other 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to Use {*} and {**} Operators in APIRule v2alp...
entry.content:
Dear Abhinav,

Make sure that you are using the latest release of the API Gateway module. In case you are using BTP Kyma runtime, the release that includes support for those operators (2.9.0) did not reach regular channel yet at this time.

@VaradBhagwat

Has the app been deployed to Cloud Foundry? Also, can you insert records into HANA Cloud with a post request from a REST client (such as Postman)?

entry.title: Re: How can I archive the Performance and Goals forms via API?
entry.content: Hi, bumping this, as I also need to extract this data for achive purpose through API. Can we trigger some PDF generation through the API and receive the PDF as attachment data through API? Or do we have to query all the data and populate/generate a PDF in groovy?

entry.title: Delegation for Performance Reviews 2H 2024 Release Feature Target Population
entry.content:
Dear SAP community, 

We are having some issues with setting up the target population to maximize the PMGM Delegation Feature efficiently. 

Ideally, the permissions should be set up as below:

Delegation Management Permission (delegators)

e.g. Admin -> Managers

Delegator-Delegatee Relationship -> (delegatees)
e.g. Managers - Managers

Delegator-Employee Relationship (Subject User of the form to be delegated)
e.g. HR Admin - all
e.g. Managers - Direct Reports

For the first two permission roles wherein the target population is Managers, the question is how can we group all of them together as usually they cannot be grouped using a people pool filter as they are random managers assigned to employees. How are we able to go about this? 

Please advise. 

Thanks and Regards,

Kat

entry.title: 2nd SAP Meetup in Thessaloniki
entry.content:
Come and join the second SAP meetup in Thessaloniki!

If you need any kind of information drop us an email at sapthessaloniki@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: QS61 how it works
entry.content:
@ps0410  You can use copy result functionalities to copy result from previous inspection lot.

you can refer this link Copying result from prevoius inspection Lot - SAP Community and Solved: copy results from material A to material B (batch ... - SAP Community for more.

Reach out to me for more details.

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between SOAP RM and SOAP 1. in...
entry.content:
Hi Upendra,

1. SOAP 1.x Adapter

Purpose:

Used for standard SOAP-based communication.

Supports SOAP 1.1 and SOAP 1.2 protocols.


Features:

Allows synchronous and asynchronous message exchange patterns.

Commonly used for Web Services Description Language (WSDL)-based web services.

Offers various authentication methods like HTTP Basic, OAuth, and Client Certificate.

Supports attachments (MIME, MTOM).

Typically used for lightweight, stateless communications.


Use Case:

When integrating with SOAP-based web services that do not require guaranteed delivery or message persistence.

For scenarios where the communication is synchronous, and the external system expects a real-time response.

---

2. SOAP RM Adapter (Reliable Messaging)

Purpose:

An extension of the SOAP protocol that ensures reliable delivery of messages.

Built on WS-ReliableMessaging (WS-RM) standards.


Features:

Guarantees message delivery even in case of communication failures (e.g., network issues).

Includes message persistence, retries, and acknowledgment.

Ensures ordered delivery and duplicates prevention (if configured).

Typically used for asynchronous communication where reliability is critical.


Use Case:

When you need guaranteed delivery of SOAP messages to the target system.

For scenarios requiring transactional integrity, such as financial transactions or critical data exchanges.

---

How to Choose Between SOAP RM and SOAP 1.x in SAP CPI ?

Use SOAP RM Adapter:

If the integration scenario requires reliable delivery, retries, and message persistence.

When interacting with systems supporting WS-RM and reliability is essential (e.g., SAP systems like S/4HANA).

Asynchronous scenarios where the sender system doesn't need immediate responses.


Use SOAP 1.x Adapter:

For standard SOAP-based communication with less complexity.

When the web service being called doesn’t support WS-ReliableMessaging.

For synchronous message exchanges or lightweight integrations where reliability isn't a concern.

---

Key Differences:

---

Configuration Perspective in SAP CPI

SOAP RM Adapter: Choose this in the adapter configuration when the target system supports WS-RM and you need reliability.

SOAP 1.x Adapter: Use this for generic SOAP services that don’t need WS-RM.

entry.title: Re: Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Journal Entries: A Guide to Business Contexts and Rules
entry.content:
Hi Ciara,

I cannot create any rule with 'Market Segment' Business Content or Event in on-premise implementation.

However, even the first example,e 'Set Profit Center', was created using 'Market Segment.'

How can we activate this event and be able to create a rule?

Our version is SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP FIORI FOR SAP AC 2.0

entry.title: Re: Activate Generic Object Services for PR
entry.content:
You could try

  • BAdI GOS_SRV_SELECT and GOS_MULT_PUBLISH to force display and activation of the option
    Object type BUS2105 - Service SRELATIONS 
  • SU53 for authorizations
  • Debug of CL_GOS_SRV_RELATIONS

entry.title: Re: SAP integration suite data in excel sheet
entry.content:
Hi Sohail,

Please use the iflow design as a starting point and to take design idea/ inspirations to achieve your requirements. If you directly use the iflow as is, it might not suffice.

entry.title: Re: WM Bin sort
entry.content:
Hello @sap_mm31 

You won't be able to define such a sorting in start because your stack level is defined with 2 or 3 chars, e.g. 00, 14, 100. 

One option is to change storage bin numbering so the stack level is always defined with 3 chars, i.e. 000, 014, 100. Then standard sorting will work.

Another option is to update LAGP-SORLP field with a custom report. This way you can define sorting which ever way you need.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Copy sequence not workinq from AO only RSPLAN trace mode, BPC 11.1 Embedded Model BW4/HANA
entry.content: Hello Christina. We are facing the same issue. Did you manage to solve this?

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: CDS views that can be used to Distinguish fields chan...
entry.content: Hi
